heading: Permitting for international bridges
label: 535d Permitting for international bridges

Prior ProvisionsA prior section 535d, , , , directed that tolls charged for use of an international bridge constructed or acquired under this subchapter by private individual, company, or other private entity be collected for a reasonable period for amortization of construction or acquisition costs, plus interest and reasonable return, that at end of such period the United States portion of bridge become the property of the State having jurisdiction over such United States portion, and that accurate records on expenditures and tolls collected be kept and annually reported to Secretary of Transportation, with authority for Secretary to conduct audits, prior to repeal by , , .Pub. children.label: A
children.text: 1 or more international bridges in Webb County, Texas.

children.label: B
children.text: An international bridge in Cameron County, Texas.

children.label: C
children.text: An international bridge in Maverick County, Texas.

children.label: i
children.text: an approval by the President to construct, maintain, and operate an international bridge under ; orsection 535b of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: an approval by the President to construct, maintain, and operate an international bridge pursuant to a process described in Executive Order 13867 (84 Fed. Reg. 15491; relating to Issuance of Permits With Respect to Facilities and Land Transportation Crossings at the International Boundaries of the United States) (or any successor Executive Order).

children.heading: Inclusion
children.label: B Inclusion
children.text: The term “Presidential permit” includes an amendment to an approval described in clause (i) or (ii) of subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Secretary
children.label: 3 Secretary
children.text: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of State.

children.heading: Application
children.label: b Application
children.text: An eligible applicant for a Presidential permit to construct, maintain, and operate an international bridge shall submit an application for the permit to the Secretary.

children.label: A
children.text: to grant the Presidential permit; or

children.label: B
children.text: to deny the Presidential permit.

children.heading: Consideration
children.label: 2 Consideration
children.text: The basis for a recommendation under paragraph (1) shall be whether the international bridge is in the foreign policy interests of the United States.

children.label: A
children.text: the date on which the Secretary makes a recommendation under subsection (c)(1); and

children.label: B
children.text: the date on which the Secretary is required to make a recommendation under subsection (c)(1).

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), if the President does not grant or deny the Presidential permit for an application under subsection (b) by the deadline described in paragraph (1), the Presidential permit shall be considered to have been granted as of that deadline.

children.heading: Requirement
children.label: B Requirement
children.text: As a condition on a Presidential permit considered to be granted under subparagraph (A), the eligible applicant shall complete all applicable environmental documents required pursuant to ( et seq.).Public Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321
to include in the application under subsection (b) environmental documents prepared pursuant to ( et seq.); orPublic Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321
to have completed any environmental review under ( et seq.) prior to the President granting a Presidential permit under subsection (d).Public Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321

prohibits the President from granting a Presidential permit conditioned on the eligible applicant completing all environmental documents pursuant to ( et seq.) and complying with relevant laws;Public Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321

prohibits the Secretary from requesting a list of all permits and approvals from Federal, State, and local agencies that the eligible applicant believes are required in connection with the international bridge, or a brief description of how those permits and approvals will be acquired prior to making a recommendation to the President;
exempts an eligible applicant from the requirement to complete all environmental documents pursuant to ( et seq.) prior to construction of an international bridge; orPublic Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321
exempts an eligible applicant from complying with ( et seq.) or any other law.Public Law 91–19042 U.S.C. 4321
